value: Beads memories DO sync via the gitops JSONL flow used by mattermost-taskbridge. Verified empirically on bd 0.61.0: 'kv.memory.<slug>' rows live in the config table; the config table is exported to .beads/backup/config.jsonl by 'bd backup'; that file is committed to git and reaches the cluster pod via the sync loop. So memories travel with everything else, just hidden inside config.jsonl rather than a memories.jsonl. After the v1.0.0 migration (epic obsidian-mcp-server-7q9, ADR obsidian-mcp-server-pin), the same is true via 'bd export'/'bd import' which round-trips memories as {"_type":"memory",...} JSONL lines (added in 0.62.0). Earlier-me incorrectly told the user memories don't sync — that was based on grepping for 'memories.jsonl' instead of grepping config.jsonl for 'kv.memory'. Lesson: verify storage location before claiming a sync gap.

lefthook + beads cannot share core.hooksPath. Discovered while fixing obsidian-mcp-server-2xp. Both tools want to own git's hook path. Symptoms in order: (1) lefthook install refuses to clobber when bd already set core.hooksPath = .beads/hooks → bun install fails in the prepare script; (2) calling 'lefthook run pre-commit' from inside a beads hook script triggers lefthook's auto-sync on every invocation, which then writes its own shim into .beads/hooks/pre-commit and renames the original to pre-commit.old — same conflict, inverted. The ONLY stable arrangement is to never let the lefthook binary execute as part of the git hook flow at all. Fix landed in commit e08738b: inlined oxlint + dprint check directly into .beads/hooks/pre-commit (outside the bd BEGIN/END markers), keeping lefthook.yml only as a config artefact / documentation. Do not try options (a)-(d) from the bd 2xp description — they all eventually re-trigger the auto-sync war.
